Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 1. Understand the Goal: The objective is to analyze a provided event log of a Procure-to-Pay P2P process identify anomalies deviations from standard best practices and explain why they are problematic based on general business knowledge. 2. Understand the Standard P2P Process: What is the normal flow? 1. Need Identification Create Purchase Requisition PR. 2. Approval Approve Purchase Requisition. 3. Ordering Issue Purchase Order PO. 4. Receipt Receive Goods. 5. Invoicing Vendor Invoice Received. 6. Verification Match Invoice to PO GRN Three-Way Match. 7. Payment Release Payment. 8. Closure Close Case. Key Controls: Approval must happen before ordering. PO must exist before goods are received ideally and definitely before payment. Goods receipt usually happens before or around the same time as invoicing but payment should happen after verification. Three-way matching PO Goods Receipt Invoice is crucial for fraud prevention and accuracy. Payment should not happen before the invoice is received and matched. Payment should not happen before goods are received unless it is a specific pre-payment scenario but standard P2P implies goods services first.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 3. Analyze Each Case Individually: Case 1001: PR Created Approve Purchase Requisition PO Issued Goods Received Invoice Received Match Payment Close. Timeline 2/1 to 2/7. Flow Logical Approval before PO PO before Goods Invoice after Goods Match before Payment. Verdict Normal. Case 1002: PR Created 08:30 PO Issued 08:40 Invoice Received 07:00 next day Payment Released 07:15 next day PR Approved 08:00 next day Goods Received 09:00 next day Match 08:00 day after Close. Anomalies: 1. PO before Approval PO issued at 08:40 PR Approved at 08:00 next day major control failure. 2. Payment before Invoice Match Payment released at 07:15 Match at 08:00 next day Paying before verifying risky. 3. Payment before Goods Receipt Payment Feb 3 Goods Feb 4 Paying before receiving high risk. 4. Invoice before Goods though sometimes okay usually goods first. 5. Approval after PO. Verdict Highly Anomalous Problematic. Case 1003: PR Created Approve PR PO Issued Payment Released Invoice Received Goods Received Match Close. Timeline Payment Feb 8 Invoice Feb 9 Goods Feb 10. Anomalies: 1. Payment before Invoice Payment Feb 8 Invoice Feb 9 pay without invoice blind payment potential fraud. 2. Payment before Goods Feb 8 to Feb 10 risky. 3. Payment before Matching Feb 8 to Feb 11 without verification. Verdict Highly Anomalous Problematic. Case 1004: PR Created Approve PR Goods Received PO Issued Invoice Match Payment Close. Timeline Goods Feb 6 09:00 PO Feb 6 09:15. Anomalies: 1. Goods before PO receiving before PO exists control violation maverick buying makes matching difficult. Verdict Anomalous Problematic.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 5. Refine the Analysis Self-Correction Double Check: Check Case 1002 again PR Approved Feb 3 08:00 PO Issued Feb 1 08:40 Yes PO before Approval Payment Feb 3 07:15 Match Feb 5 08:00 Payment before Match Goods Feb 4 Payment before Goods. Check Case 1003 again Payment Feb 8 Invoice Feb 9 Goods Feb 10 Payment in Advance without documentation Very suspicious. Check Case 1004 again Goods Feb 6 09:00 PO Feb 6 09:15 Receipt before Order Maverick Spend. Terminology Three-Way Match Maverick Buying Segregation of Duties Control Environment.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: 4. Synthesize and Structure the Output: Start with brief introduction standard P2P flow Go through each Case 1001-1004 Normal case 1001 baseline Anomalous cases 1002 1003 1004 list violations explain Risk Problem Fraud Financial Loss Compliance Operational Efficiency Conclude summary risks Segregation of Duties Control Failures etc. 6. Drafting the Response Mental Outline Intro Define standard flow Case 1001 Green light Case 1002 Red light PO before Approval Payment before Match Goods Case 1003 Red light Payment before Invoice Goods Match Case 1004 Orange Red light Goods before PO Summary Categorize risks Fraud Compliance Operational. 7. Final Polish Ensure clear formatting bullet points bold text readability explanation links anomaly to business risk e g Why Payment before Invoice bad Risk of overpayment or fraud.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 8. Final Review against Prompt: Did I identify anomalies Yes Did I explain why they are problematic Yes Did I rely on general P2P knowledge Yes This thought process moves from understanding the domain to specific data analysis then to risk assessment and finally to structured communication.
